title: wedelia calycina var. parviflora
intro: Wedelia calycina var. parviflora, also known as the small-flowered wedelia or parviflora wedelia, is a flowering plant species of the daisy family (Asteraceae), native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The medicinal properties of Wedelia calycina var. parviflora include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Traditionally, Wedelia calycina var. parviflora is prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
